text:
Once upon a time, there was a little mouse.

As little mice tend to do, this one loved living close to the old oak tree. Every dawn, she would glide across the dew-speckled leaves. But one summer day, an awful storm blew through. Massive drops pelted like angry owls.

And then, nothing. With no lights to guide her home, the little mouse stayed put. Days stretched into nights. Eventually, hunger gnawed at her bones.

She ventured out. No stars. No moon. Just darkness.

Then she saw a path. Familiar. Never forgotten. Direct. Up she climbed.

And beyond the stars.

It was a home, though half-swallowed by clouds. Birds sang. Leaves rustled.

And the little mouse went to sleep. And dreams of oaken doors filled her mouth.

Next morning, she woke. Yes. Home. But the birds seemed a bit bigger. Something odd—

The door. No holes. Just a
